topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cardboard box sat on my doorstep like an accusation. It was addressed to "Apartment 3B," the unit I had vacated three weeks prior. My jaw tightened, the muscles in my neck corded with tension. I stared at the label, the handwriting—a messy scrawl I vaguely remembered from the previous lease agreement—seeming to mock me. My knuckles were white where I gripped the doorframe. Someone had clearly made a grave error, a profound lapse in judgment, to deliver this to me. I felt a hot flush creep up my chest, and I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to keep the tremor out of my voice when I finally snatched the package. The tape felt cheap, the contents heavy, and a simmering resentment began to bubble just beneath my skin.

[story 2]

When the delivery person handed over the parcel, I didn't offer a polite acknowledgment. My eyes locked onto the shipping label, tracing the faded ink with a deliberate, slow movement. A low growl escaped my throat, a sound I barely recognized. This was an invasion, a blatant disregard for boundaries. I physically recoiled, stepping back as if the package itself were contaminated. My hands clenched into fists so tight my nails bit into my palms. I circled the box twice, my gaze sharp and unforgiving, before snatching it up. A sharp, unpleasant feeling settled in my stomach, a churning mix of disbelief and pure indignation. I needed to find out who had made this colossal mistake.

[story 3]

I stood on the welcome mat, the afternoon sun glinting off the glossy surface of the package. My breath hitched. The address confirmed my worst suspicions: this belonged to the tenant who had moved out last month. A wave of intense displeasure washed over me, making my vision narrow slightly. I let out a sharp, involuntary sigh, the sound laced with pure annoyance. I didn't want it. I didn't want *anything* from that previous tenancy. My posture stiffened, every line of my body radiating a cold, hard refusal. With a swift, jerky motion, I bent down, picked up the offending item, and retreated quickly into my apartment, slamming the door with unnecessary force.
